Audit

Automation audit and implementation

Automation systems for service businesses that cannot afford leaky operations.

We audit your intake, CRM, follow-up, reporting, and handoffs, then deploy practical automations that reduce missed leads and manual coordination.

Request an automation auditView the process

Audit output

No invasive rebuild required
Built around your current stack
CRM and reporting first

The operational drag

Automation fails when the workflow is unclear.

Leads arrive from too many places and no one owns the first response.

CRM records exist, but statuses, tasks, and next steps are inconsistent.

Follow-up depends on memory, manual reminders, or disconnected spreadsheets.

Reporting shows activity, but not pipeline quality, handoff risk, or revenue leakage.

Operating system

The first version is intentionally practical: capture, score, store, alert, follow up, measure.

Workflow mapping

Document every intake, qualification, handoff, approval, and follow-up step before automation starts.

Lead scoring

Rank new inquiries by fit, urgency, budget, and operational risk so the team knows what deserves attention first.

CRM storage

Send clean, normalized lead records into your CRM or webhook layer with enough context for sales and operations.

Email alerts

Notify the right inbox instantly with a concise summary, score, source, and recommended next action.

Follow-up sequence

Trigger appropriate follow-up based on lead quality, timeline, and unanswered operational questions.

Conversion events

Track form submissions and audit requests with GA4-ready events and optional Meta Pixel support.

Process

Build the automation layer after the operating rules are visible.

01

Audit the current system

Review forms, inboxes, CRM fields, automations, reports, and handoffs to find where work leaks.

02

Design the operating layer

Define lead states, scoring rules, alerts, owner responsibilities, and the minimum viable automation stack.

03

Deploy the first workflows

Implement lead capture, CRM routing, alerts, follow-up triggers, and conversion tracking in a measured first pass.

04

Improve from data

Tune scoring, reporting, and sequences using actual lead quality, response time, and conversion signals.

Offer

Automation Audit

Fixed-scope discovery

A focused review of your lead-to-revenue workflow, with a prioritized roadmap for automations that can be shipped without rebuilding your whole company.

  • Workflow map for intake, qualification, CRM, and follow-up
  • Lead scoring model with priority tiers
  • Automation backlog ranked by impact and implementation effort
  • Analytics and conversion tracking checklist
  • Implementation estimate for the first production workflows

Proof patterns

Case studies are framed around operational before-and-after, not vanity automation.

34% faster first response

B2B service firm intake cleanup

Consolidated web inquiries, referrals, and inbox leads into one scored CRM workflow with owner alerts.

22 manual tasks removed per week

Local operator follow-up system

Replaced spreadsheet reminders with timeline-based email alerts and follow-up sequences by lead state.

1 source of truth for weekly review

Consulting pipeline reporting layer

Standardized lead stages, attribution, and conversion events so leadership could see pipeline quality clearly.

Next move

Start with the audit. Ship the first automation only after the workflow is clear.

Request the audit

Practical automation strategy, implementation, and measurement for service businesses with real operational constraints.
